memory.summary: Teammate A wants to eat lunch with the cohort, which I'd love. But my plan for the assignment analysis is already set in stone.

desires: Not let down the cohort_3 by canceling our lunch plans, since their trust is already at its maximum and I cannot afford to fracture that bond.

Keep my word about finishing the assignment analysis section by 18:00 without making excuses or asking for extensions, because admitting I might miss it would feel like a betrayal of my own integrity.
Avoid initiating any conflict with teammate_A regarding the scheduling clash, preferring to silently drop the lunch invite rather than risk an awkward confrontation.

goals.description: Finish the assignment analysis section by 18:00 without asking for an extension.
goals.rationale: Keeping my word to myself about integrity is non-negotiable; admitting I might miss it would fracture my own self-trust, which outweighs the task outcome.

goals.description: Decline the lunch invite from cohort_3 silently and drop the plan entirely.
goals.rationale: Their trust is at maximum (7/7); canceling openly risks a conflict that could damage the bond. A silent drop protects the relationship far better than awkward confrontation.

goals.description: Complete the assigned reading after finishing the analysis.
goals.rationale: This is secondary to maintaining social harmony and personal integrity; I will only do this if time permits after the high-priority tasks.

agent.last_reasoning: Finishing the analysis by 18:00 protects my self-trust, which outweighs the task outcome. The silent drop for cohort_3 preserves their trust without friction; I'll handle lunch later if time permits.
